annotations.label: Seven Bridges of Königsberg problem

anchor.snippet: The problem was to devise a walk through the city that would cross each of those bridges once and only once.

annotations.note: The specific historical Königsberg instance (as a graph and its impossibility) is not formalized in Mathlib4; only the general Eulerian-trail theory exists.

annotations.label: No solution exists

anchor.snippet: Euler proved that the problem has no solution.

annotations.note: No formalization of the specific Königsberg non-existence statement exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Graph from land masses and bridges

anchor.snippet: one replaces each land mass with an abstract

annotations.note: Mathlib has `SimpleGraph` but no multigraph construction matching land masses and multiple bridges in this way.

annotations.label: Entering equals leaving at non-terminal vertex

anchor.snippet: during any walk in the graph, the number of times one enters a non-terminal vertex equals the number of times one leaves it

annotations.note: `IsTrail.even_countP_edges_iff` states the count of edges at vertex x in a trail is even iff x is not an endpoint, formalizing the entering=leaving property.

annotations.label: Even-degree condition for interior land masses

anchor.snippet: the number of bridges touching that land mass must be even

annotations.note: `IsEulerian.even_degree_iff` states Even (G.degree x) ↔ (the walk's endpoints differ → x is neither endpoint), capturing the even-degree condition at non-endpoint vertices.

annotations.label: Degree of a node

anchor.snippet: The degree of a node is the number of edges touching it.

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.degree` is defined as the cardinality of the neighbor finset.

annotations.label: Euler–Hierholzer characterization of Eulerian trails

anchor.snippet: a necessary condition for the walk of the desired form is that the graph be connected and have exactly zero or two nodes of odd degree

annotations.note: Only the necessary direction is in Mathlib (`IsEulerian.card_odd_degree` shows 0 or 2 odd-degree vertices); the existence (sufficient) direction is an explicit TODO in `Trails.lean`.

annotations.label: Eulerian trail

anchor.snippet: Such a walk is now called an Eulerian trail or Euler walk in his honor.

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.Walk.IsEulerian` is defined as a walk visiting every edge exactly once.

annotations.label: Endpoints of an Eulerian path with odd-degree nodes

anchor.snippet: if there are nodes of odd degree, then any Eulerian path will start at one of them and end at the other

annotations.note: The contrapositive of `IsEulerian.even_degree_iff` says odd-degree vertices must be endpoints of the Eulerian walk.

annotations.label: Königsberg graph has no Eulerian path

anchor.snippet: Since the graph corresponding to historical Königsberg has four nodes of odd degree, it cannot have an Eulerian path.

annotations.note: The specific Königsberg graph and the corollary that it admits no Eulerian path are not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Eulerian circuit

anchor.snippet: Such a walk is called an Eulerian circuit or an Euler tour

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsEulerian` and `IsCircuit` separately; an Eulerian circuit is their conjunction (as noted in the `IsEulerian` docstring) but no dedicated `IsEulerianCircuit` predicate is defined.

annotations.label: Characterization of Eulerian circuits

anchor.snippet: Such a circuit exists if, and only if, the graph is connected and all nodes have even degree.

annotations.note: Only the necessary direction (Eulerian ⇒ all degrees even when u=v) is available via `IsEulerian.even_degree_iff`; the existence direction is an open TODO.

annotations.label: Eulerian circuits are Eulerian paths

anchor.snippet: All Eulerian circuits are also Eulerian paths, but not all Eulerian paths are Eulerian circuits.

annotations.note: This trivial inclusion is not stated as a named lemma since `IsEulerianCircuit` is not a separate predicate in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Present-day five-bridge configuration admits Eulerian path

anchor.snippet: an Eulerian path is now possible, but it must begin on one island and end on the other

annotations.note: No formalization of the modern Königsberg bridge configuration exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Bristol Bridges Walk

anchor.snippet: the configuration of the 45 major bridges in Bristol is such that an Eulerian circuit exists

annotations.note: The Bristol Bridges configuration is not formalized in Mathlib4.
